sessions.intent: Design test suite for Piece 8: Builder Module

before.assumptions: Architect plan at v1/plan.md defines GoalFile parser, 8 builder actions, Step.Merge, Goal.MergeFrom. No builder module code exists yet — tests define the contract.
before.risk: GoalFile is a new parser (unusual for PLang which is LLM-based) — needs thorough edge case coverage. Actions depend on engine.Modules reflection which may be tricky to test.

sessions.plan: 49 test stubs across 6 batches: GoalFile parser (12), Step.Merge/Goal.MergeFrom (7), getActions/getTypeInfo (7), getGoals/validateActions (9), app/save/merge/guard (8), PLang integration (6). Independent additions: tabs, blank lines, conditional error replacement, cacheable flag, empty folder, building guard.

actions.context: 43 C# test stubs for builder module — GoalFile, merge, actions, validation, app, save
6 PLang integration test stubs for builder module

after.notes: 49 test stubs written. Ready for coder bot to implement builder module and make tests pass.

sessions.intent: Address gaps found in fresh-eyes review of v1 test stubs

before.assumptions: v1 has 49 stubs but missing line number tests, PrPath derivation, corrupt .pr resilience, per-action building guards, and PLang stubs are too thin for coder to work from.
before.risk: Low — additive changes only, no existing tests modified.

sessions.plan: Add 10 C# test stubs (2 GoalFile, 1 GetGoals, 7 net BuildingGuard) and flesh out 4 PLang tests with concrete step shapes. Total 49 → 59.

actions.context: Added 10 C# test stubs: line numbers, PrPath derivation, corrupt .pr resilience, 8 per-action building guards
Fleshed out 4 PLang tests with concrete steps, marked 2 as deferred to C#

after.notes: 59 total test stubs (53 C# + 6 PLang). All gaps addressed except engine.RunAction I/O pattern (implementation detail for coder).

sessions.intent: Implement builder module: GoalFile, 8 actions, Step.Merge, Goal.MergeFrom, tests

before.assumptions: Architect plan defines 8 actions + GoalFile. Test stubs exist (53 C# + 6 PLang). Zero builder module code exists. v1 PlangModule/Program.cs has reference implementations for all actions.
before.risk: GoalFile is the only real parser in PLang — edge cases around blank lines, multi-line comments, continuation lines. Builder actions that use engine.RunAction for file I/O need careful context wiring. Building guard needs to be checked on every action entry.

sessions.plan: Phase 1: Add Step.Merge() and Goal.MergeFrom() to entity classes. Phase 2: GoalFile parser (.goal text → List<Goal>). Phase 3: 8 builder actions (getActions, getTypeInfo, getGoals, validateActions, mergeStep, getApp, saveApp, saveGoals). Phase 4: Implement 53 C# test stubs.

actions.context: Added Step.Merge() and Goal.MergeFrom() methods — OBP ownership of merge logic
11 new files: builder provider + 8 builder actions + type record. Standard handler→provider delegation pattern.
Implemented all 53 C# test stubs. Fixed alias conflicts with global usings.

after.notes: All 2015 tests pass (0 failures). 9 new source files, 2 modified entity files, 10 test files implemented. Ready for codeanalyzer review.

before.assumptions: Builder module implements handler→provider delegation. Goal.Parse() is the only real text parser in PLang. Code uses engine.RunAction for file I/O. All tests reportedly pass.
before.risk: Goal.Parse() is ~150 lines of text parsing — unusual for PLang, needs careful scrutiny. DefaultBuilderProvider at 344 lines is the largest single provider. Error handling patterns need audit.

sessions.plan: 5-pass analysis: OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, deletion test. Focus on DefaultBuilderProvider.cs (core logic), Goal.Parse() (parser), engine modifications (Modules.Describe, TypeMapping additions).

actions.context: 5-pass analysis of 15 production files + 10 test files. Verdict: NEEDS WORK — 5 minor findings, 0 major.

after.notes: NEEDS WORK — 5 minor findings: 2 untested Parse() paths, unguarded Activator.CreateInstance, untested IConfigure<T> defaults, Runtime1 type in FormatForLlm. Send to coder for fixes.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder fixes for v1 findings

before.assumptions: Coder addressed 4 of 5 findings in one commit. 3 new tests, 2 small production code changes.
before.risk: Low — fixes are small and targeted.

sessions.plan: Verify each fix against the original finding. Full review of fix-introduced code (3 new tests, try/catch guard, file read error reporting).

actions.context: Re-review: 4/5 findings resolved, fix-introduced code clean. PASS.

after.notes: PASS — all findings resolved (1 deferred as pre-existing). Recommend tester next.

sessions.intent: Fresh-eyes re-analysis of builder module — find what v1/v2 missed

before.assumptions: v1/v2 found and resolved 5 minor findings. Coder fixes verified. User wants fresh eyes to catch anything missed.
before.risk: Medium — the first review may have anchored on specific areas and missed broader patterns.

sessions.plan: Re-read all production code from scratch. Apply all 5 passes without anchoring on v1 findings. Focus on clone family audit, Describe() reflection gaps, dead code paths.

actions.context: Fresh-eyes analysis found 2 missed findings: Describe() leaks [Provider] properties (medium), Step.Clone() drops Action fields (minor). Verdict: NEEDS WORK.

after.notes: NEEDS WORK — 2 new findings: Describe() leaks [Provider] properties (affects all modules), Step.Clone() drops Action fields. Send to coder.

sessions.intent: Verify coder fixes for v3 findings

before.assumptions: Coder addressed all 3 v3 findings in one commit.
before.risk: Low — fixes are minimal and mechanical.

sessions.plan: Verify each fix against the original finding. Review fix-introduced code.

after.notes: PASS — all v3 findings resolved. Recommend tester next.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of builder module (coder v1)

before.assumptions: 53 C# tests all pass. Codeanalyzer v4 passed. Coverage should be high for builder module.
before.risk: Builder module is new code with new test files — highest risk for false-greens.

sessions.plan: Run C# tests, collect coverage, read all 10 test files + production code for false-green hunting, check PLang test quality, write test-report.json.

actions.context: Test quality analysis: 2018 pass, 0 fail. Found 3 major false-greens (existence-not-correctness pattern), 4 minor findings. Verdict: NEEDS FIXES.

after.notes: NEEDS FIXES — 3 major false-greens in ValidateActions, SaveApp, SaveGoals tests. Send back to coder.

sessions.intent: Re-verify builder module tests after coder fixes

before.assumptions: Coder fixed all 7 findings + 1 production bug. Need to verify fixes are honest.
before.risk: Low — targeted fixes, not a rewrite.

sessions.plan: Run tests, run coverage, verify each fix addresses the original finding, fresh-eyes pass for anything missed.

actions.context: All 7 findings resolved + 1 production bug fixed. Coverage improved. Verdict: PASS.

after.notes: PASS — all findings resolved, 2022 tests pass, coverage strong. Recommend security analyst next.

before.assumptions: Builder module is new code with 8 actions, Goal.Parse() text parser, provider/module registry changes. All tests pass, codeanalyzer and tester both passed.
before.risk: Goal.Parse() is a text parser — unusual for PLang. Provider registry has type resolution logic. Assembly loading paths exist.

sessions.plan: Blue team: map 7 attack surface areas (goal-parser, pr-deser, action-registry, provider-registry, assembly-loading, builder-auth, merge-pipeline). Red team: assess each vector for exploitability under PLang threat model. Write security-report.json and verdict.

actions.context: Full security review of 21 source files. 7 attack surface areas mapped. 5 findings: 0 critical, 0 high, 2 medium, 3 low.
Security report, plan, summary, verdict. PASS.

after.notes: PASS — 0 critical, 0 high, 2 medium, 3 low. Builder module follows PLang threat model correctly. Recommend auditor next.
